steps.title: Translate the percentages into equations
steps.content:
Let U = undergraduate students, G = graduate students, and P = postdoctoral students.

From the problem:
  • Undergraduate = 71.5% of postdoctoral: $U = 0.715P$
  • Graduate = 45% of undergraduate: $G = 0.45U$
  • Given: $G = 6,435$
We need $U + P$.

steps.title: Find undergraduate students by working backwards
steps.content:
Since $G = 0.45U$ and $G = 6,435$, divide both sides by 0.45:
$$U = \frac{6,435}{0.45} = 14,300$$

steps.title: Find postdoctoral students using the first relationship
